title: Pelican Nebula & North American Nebula
description: Experimenting with new image processing techniques.

title: Heart Nebula (IC 1805)
description: Second attempt with a new scope and narrowband filter technique.

title: Iris Nebula (NGC 7023)
description: Located about 1,300 light-years away, one of the faintest objects.

title: Western Veil Nebula
description: A supernova remnant, unlike others formed from a single massive star explosion.

title: Dumbbell Nebula
description: 1,300 light-years away. A halo surrounds the luminous structure.

title: Lagoon Nebula
description: 4,000 to 5,000 light-years away, spanning 110 x 50 light-years across.

title: Great Orion Nebula (second attempt)
description: 1,300 light-years away. Second attempt at this object.

title: Bode's Galaxy
description: 12 million light-years away, spanning 90 thousand light-years across.

title: Jellyfish Nebula
description: A supernova remnant roughly 5,000 light-years away.

title: Star Cluster in Hercules (M13)
description: Hundreds of thousands of stars in a globular cluster.

title: Crab Nebula
description: A supernova remnant around 6,500 light-years away.

title: Monkey Head Nebula
description: In the constellation of Orion, around 6,400 light-years away.

title: Heart Nebula (mosaic)
description: 7,500 light-years away. If visible to the naked eye, it would be around 4 times the moon's size.

title: Rosette Nebula
description: Thousands of stars in the center were created from the matter of this nebula.

title: Horsehead Nebula
description: Another gem hidden in the constellation of Orion.

title: Great Orion Nebula
description: Around 1,300 light-years away in the constellation of Orion, also known as Mahavyadha — the great hunter in Sanskrit.
